relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: A Sabbath meal becomes a watched space.
A man in visible need stands before Jesus.
Jesus asks whether Sabbath permits healing.
Silence gives way to healing and release.
Mercy pulls child or ox from the well on Sabbath.
They cannot answer mercy.
Jesus notices status-seeking at the table.
Do not seize the first place.
Self-exaltation can end in shame.
The last place leaves room for gift.
Exaltation and humility reverse.
Hospitality aimed at repayment stays trapped in exchange.
The banquet of the reign welcomes those without repayment power.
Blessing comes when giving cannot be paid back.
Someone blesses eating bread in the Divine reign.
A great dinner is prepared and many are invited.
The ready feast calls the invited.
A field becomes excuse.
Oxen become excuse.
Marriage becomes excuse.
The invitation turns toward the poor, disabled, blind, and lame.
There is still room.
The house must be filled from roads and hedges.
Those who refuse the invitation miss the feast.
Great crowds require honest discipleship teaching.
Following reorders even the deepest family and self-attachments.
The disciple carries the cross after him.
A tower asks for cost counted first.
Unfinished foundation becomes public mockery.
Beginning without finishing is exposed.
A king must deliberate before battle.
Peace is sought before ruin when strength is lacking.
Possessions must lose their claim over discipleship.
Salt is good when it remains salty.
Ears are called again to hear.
